children.heading: Report on Small Business and Competition
children.label: a Report on Small Business and Competition

children.text: examine the current role of small business in the economy on an industry-by-industry basis;

present current and historical data on production, employment, investment, population, job creation and retention, annual business failures, annual business startups, and other economic variables for small business in the economy as a whole and for small business in each sector of the economy, with, to the extent practicable, specific statistics divided as to urban, suburban, and rural areas;
identify economic trends which will or may affect the small business sector and the state of competition;

examine the effects on small business and competition of policies, programs, and activities, including, but not limited to the Internal Revenue Code [ et seq.], the Employee Retirement Income Security Act [ et seq.], the Securities Act of 1933 [ et seq.], and the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 [ et seq.], and regulations promulgated thereunder; identify problems generated by such policies, programs, and activities; and recommend legislative and administrative solutions to such problems; and26 U.S.C. 129 U.S.C. 100115 U.S.C. 77a15 U.S.C. 78a
recommend a program for carrying out the policy declared in , together with such recommendations for legislation as he may deem necessary or desirable.section 631a of this title

children.heading: Appendix to report
children.label: b Appendix to report
children.text: The President also shall transmit simultaneously as an appendix to such annual report, a report, by agency and department, on the total dollar value of all Federal contracts exceeding $10,000 in amount and the dollar amount (including the subcontracts thereunder in excess of $10,000) awarded to small, minority-owned, female-owned, and veteran-owned businesses.

children.heading: Supplementary reports
children.label: c Supplementary reports
children.text: The President may transmit from time to time to the Congress reports supplementary to the Report on Small Business and Competition, each of which shall include such supplementary or revised recommendations as he may deem necessary or desirable to achieve the policy declared in .section 631a of this title

children.heading: Referral to Congressional committees
children.label: d Referral to Congressional committees
children.text: The Report on Small Business and Competition and all supplementary reports transmitted under subsections (b) and (c) of this section shall, when transmitted to Congress, be referred to the Senate Select Committee on Small Business and the Committee on Small Business of the House of Representatives.

children.text: small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals, by gender, as defined pursuant to ;section 637(d) of this title
small business concerns owned and controlled by women; and
qualified HUBZone small business concern (as defined in section 632(p) of this title).23So in original. Two pars. (3) have been enacted.2See References in Text note below.3
